title: PDF Upload & Extraction
description: Stand up the backend Express API with TypeScript, in-memory data layer, PDF upload endpoint, and automated field extraction. By end of sprint a developer can POST a PDF and receive back a draft form schema JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Monorepo structured with /backend and /frontend packages and shared TypeScript config
Backend Express server runs on port 3000 with health-check GET /api/health returning 200 OK
POST /api/forms/upload accepts multipart/form-data with a PDF file
Uploaded PDF is stored in-memory with a unique UUID formId and createdAt timestamp
Extraction engine is triggered automatically after upload and returns a draft form schema
Draft schema conforms to: { formId, title, version, status: 'draft', fields: [{ id, label, type, required, order }] }
Supported field types extracted: text, textarea, date, number, radio, checkbox, select
POST /api/forms/upload responds with { formId, status: 'extraction-complete', draftSchemaId }
Unsupported file types (non-PDF) return 400 with a structured error JSON body
GET /api/forms returns a list of all uploaded forms with formId, title, status, createdAt
GET /api/forms/:formId returns the form with its current draft schema
All API routes return consistent JSON error shapes: { error: { code, message } }
Unit tests cover: file validation, extraction logic, schema shape validation
At least 1 sample test PDF included in /backend/test-fixtures/
README.md in /backend documents how to run the service locally

techStack.pdfExtraction: pdf-parse for text extraction; heuristic field detection

definitionOfDone: A test PDF can be uploaded via curl/Postman and a valid draft form schema JSON returned
No known OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities: file type validated server-side, filenames sanitised
README updated with local setup instructions and example curl commands
Code passes TypeScript strict mode with no errors
Evaluator must run and produce a passing evaluation-01.json before sprint is marked complete
